message:
perf(string): terminator-aware strnstr bounded absent scan (bd-ef934)

Replaces the BSD strnstr two-pass approach (strlen prescan + windows
slide) with a single forward pass bounded by min(n, haystack.len())
and short-circuited on the first NUL byte. Mirrors the streaming
strategy already landed for strstr (bd-dom7k), strpbrk, strspn,
strsep, wcsrchr, and wcsstr in recent perf slices.

Implementation (crates/frankenlibc-core/src/string/str.rs)
- Drops the strlen(haystack).min(n) prescan; instead scans up to the
  combined byte limit and treats either an in-bound NUL or running
  off the bound as immediate \"not found\".
- First-byte filter avoids entering the inner needle compare loop
  unless haystack[i] matches needle[0]; inner loop also bails on a
  NUL inside the candidate window so we never read past the
  haystack's logical end.
- Preserves the existing zero-length needle, bound-vs-needle, and
  match-at-zero contracts established by the existing tests.

Tests
- test_strnstr_ignores_match_after_bound_without_terminator: the
  needle \"ZQ\" sits past index 4 in an unterminated haystack and the
  bound n=4 must rule it out before the bytes are even examined.
- test_strnstr_unterminated_haystack_match_within_bound: when the
  match lies fully inside n bytes of an unterminated haystack the
  function still returns the right offset, exercising the \"no NUL
  before the bound\" path.

Benchmark (crates/frankenlibc-bench/benches/string_bench.rs)
- Adds bench_strnstr_bounded_absent across 16/64/256/1024/4096 byte
  bounds with a tight all-A haystack and a non-matching ZQ needle,
  using the same iter_custom + BenchStats reporting pattern as the
  surrounding scans so before/after evidence flows through the
  existing bench harness.
- Wires the new function into the criterion_group targets list.

Beads
- Adds bd-ef934 (in_progress) so this perf slice is tracked while
  the before/after benchmark proof and ABI strnstr coverage close
  out the bead.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
